title: Configuring Models
summary: Explains how to configure the main model and auxiliary models (vision, compression, session search, etc.) via the dashboard, CLI slash commands, model aliases, and REST API.

config_keys.description: Custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint for main model

slash_commands.purpose: Show or change the current model
Switch model for current session only
Switch model and persist to config.yaml

code_examples.caption: Main model config
code_examples.code:
model:
  provider: openrouter
  default: anthropic/claude-opus-4.7
  base_url: ''
  api_mode: chat_completions

code_examples.caption: Auxiliary vision override
code_examples.code:
auxiliary:
  vision:
    provider: openrouter
    model: google/gemini-2.5-flash

code_examples.caption: Model aliases
code_examples.code:
model_aliases:
  fav:
    model: claude-sonnet-4.6
    provider: anthropic
  grok:
    model: grok-4
    provider: x-ai

gotchas: Dashboard model change applies to new sessions only — use /model inside existing chat to hot-swap
Existing sessions don't re-read config.yaml for model changes — restart the gateway to force pickup
provider: auto with model: '' means use the main model for that auxiliary task
If a provider is missing from the picker, run hermes setup or add its API key in Keys
On OpenRouter, bare model names like 'claude-sonnet-4' resolve within OpenRouter's aggregator
The session token for the REST API rotates on every server restart — grab from browser devtools
full_text:
# user-guide/configuring-models

# Configuring Models

Hermes uses two kinds of model slots:

- **Main model** — what the agent thinks with.
- **Auxiliary models** — smaller side-jobs (context compression, vision, web-page summarization, session search, approval scoring, MCP tool routing, session-title generation, skill search).

## The Models page

Open the dashboard and click **Models** in the sidebar.

1. **Model Settings** — assign models to slots
2. **Usage analytics** — ranked cards with token counts, cost, capability badges

## Setting the main model

Click **Change** on the Main model row. The picker shows:
- **Left** — authenticated providers only
- **Right** — curated agentic model list for the selected provider

Type in the filter box to narrow by provider name, slug, or model ID.

Pick a model, hit **Switch**. Writes to `~/.hermes/config.yaml` under `model`. Applies to **new sessions only** — use `/model` inside an existing chat to hot-swap.

## Setting auxiliary models

Click **Show auxiliary** to reveal eight task slots. Every auxiliary task defaults to `auto` (uses main model).

### Common override patterns

| Task | When to override |
|---|---|
| **Title Gen** | Almost always — a flash model writes titles as well as Opus |
| **Vision** | When main model lacks vision (Kimi, DeepSeek) |
| **Compression** | When burning reasoning tokens on Opus/M2.7 for summarization |
| **Session Search** | When recall queries fan out — keep bill predictable |
| **Approval** | For `approval_mode: smart` — use haiku/flash/gpt-5-mini |
| **Web Extract** | When using web_extract heavily |

## The "Use as" shortcut

Every model card has a **Use as** dropdown:
- **Main model** — assign to main slot
- **All auxiliary tasks** — assign to all 8 aux slots at once
- **Individual task options** — Vision, Web Extract, Compression, etc.

## What gets written to config.yaml

**Main model:**
```yaml
model:
  provider: openrouter
  default: anthropic/claude-opus-4.7
  base_url: ''
  api_mode: chat_completions
```

**Auxiliary override:**
```yaml
auxiliary:
  vision:
    provider: openrouter
    model: google/gemini-2.5-flash
    base_url: ''
    api_key: ''
    timeout: 120
    extra_body: {}
    download_timeout: 30
```

**Auxiliary on auto (default):**
```yaml
auxiliary:
  compression:
    provider: auto
    model: ''
    base_url: ''
```

## When does it take effect?

- **CLI** (`hermes chat`): next invocation
- **Gateway**: next new session; restart gateway for immediate effect
- **Dashboard chat tab**: next new PTY; use `/model` inside existing tab

## Troubleshooting

### "No authenticated providers" in the picker
Check **Keys** in the sidebar — run `hermes setup` to wire up a provider.

### Main model didn't change in my running chat
Expected. Use `/model <name>` inside the chat to hot-swap.

### Auxiliary override "didn't take effect"
1. Did you start a new session?
2. Is `provider` set to something other than `auto`?
3. Is the provider authenticated?

## Alternative methods

### CLI slash command

```
/model gpt-5.4 --provider openrouter             # session-only
/model gpt-5.4 --provider openrouter --global    # also persists to config.yaml
```

### Custom aliases

```yaml
model_aliases:
  fav:
    model: claude-sonnet-4.6
    provider: anthropic
  grok:
    model: grok-4
    provider: x-ai
```

Or from shell: `hermes config set model.aliases.fav anthropic/claude-opus-4.6`

Then `/model fav` or `/model grok` in chat.

### `hermes model` subcommand

```bash
hermes model            # Interactive provider + model picker
```

### REST API

```bash
# List authenticated providers + curated model lists
curl -H "X-Hermes-Session-Token: $TOKEN" http://localhost:PORT/api/model/options

# Read current main + auxiliary assignments
curl -H "X-Hermes-Session-Token: $TOKEN" http://localhost:PORT/api/model/auxiliary

# Set the main model
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-Hermes-Session-Token: $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"scope":"main","provider":"openrouter","model":"anthropic/claude-opus-4.7"}' \
  http://localhost:PORT/api/model/set

# Override a single auxiliary task
curl -X POST ... \
  -d '{"scope":"auxiliary","task":"vision","provider":"openrouter","model":"google/gemini-2.5-flash"}' \
  http://localhost:PORT/api/model/set

# Reset all auxiliary tasks to auto
curl -X POST ... \
  -d '{"scope":"auxiliary","task":"__reset__","provider":"","model":""}' \
  http://localhost:PORT/api/model/set
```
